Because we can look at the difference of the models on individual data points as opposed to is opposed to looking at just the difference in the means.
One example of a statistical significance test is a bootstrap test.
And the bootstrap test is really convenient because you can implement it for any evaluation metric that you want to be using.
And so, in NLP, we can use lots of different evaluation metrics.

We can use an evaluation metric like accuracy, but we can also use an evaluation metric like F measure for classification or a blue score or character F score or word error rate or something like that.

So, the basic idea of a bootstrap test is a method that can measure p-values and confidence intervals by resampling data.
And so, the way you do this is you sample subsets from your death test set with replacement.
So, you might sample 10,000 times and you measure accuracy on these many subsets.
